questions.question: Define a partial order relation and give an example on the set {1,2,3,4}.
keyPoints: Partial order is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive
Example: divisibility relation (a|b) or ≤ relation
Show it satisfies all three properties

questions.question: Draw the Hasse diagram for the divisor poset of 12 and identify all maximal and minimal elements.
keyPoints: Hasse diagram shows covering relations: 1 covers nothing; 2,3 covered by 1; 4,6 covered by 2 and 3 respectively; 12 covered by 4 and 6
Minimal: 1; Maximal: 12

questions.question: Explain the difference between a maximal element and the greatest element in a poset with an example.
keyPoints: Maximal: no element strictly greater, may not be comparable to all
Greatest: comparable to all elements, must be unique
Example: in poset with elements {a,b,c} where a and b incomparable and both < c, both a and b are maximal but only c is greatest

questions.question: What is a lattice? Verify whether the poset (P({a,b}), ⊆) is a lattice.
keyPoints: Lattice: every pair has LUB (join) and GLB (meet)
Power set of {a,b} = {∅, {a}, {b}, {a,b}}
Check all pairs: ∅∧{a}=∅, ∅∨{a}={a}, etc.
Yes, it is a lattice (actually a boolean algebra)

questions.question: Define chain and antichain with one example each from any poset.
keyPoints: Chain: all elements comparable, for any a,b, either a≤b or b≤a
Antichain: no two distinct elements comparable
Example chain: {1,2,4} in divisibility poset of 12
Example antichain: {2,3} in divisibility poset of 12 (neither divides the other)

questions.question: In the poset (D(30), |), where D(30) is set of divisors of 30, find: (i) Upper bounds of {6,10} (ii) Lower bounds of {6,10} (iii) LUB of {6,10} (iv) GLB of {6,10}
keyPoints: Upper bounds: elements greater than or equal to both 6 and 10: {30} (and also 30 only since 6|30 and 10|30, but 15 doesn't divide 6 or 10)
Lower bounds: elements less than or equal to both: {1,2} since 1|6,1|10 and 2|6,2|10
LUB (join): 30 (least upper bound)
GLB (meet): 2 (greatest lower bound)

questions.question: Explain the covering relation in a poset and its significance in Hasse diagrams.
keyPoints: x covers y if y < x and no element z exists such that y < z < x
It represents immediate predecessor-successor relationship
In Hasse diagrams, only covering relations are drawn as edges
Transitive edges are omitted to simplify representation

questions.question: Consider the relation R on set A = {1,2,3,4,6,12} defined by aRb iff a|b. (i) Show that R is a partial order (ii) Draw its Hasse diagram (iii) Find all maximal and minimal elements (iv) Find least and greatest elements if they exist (v) Is this poset a lattice? Justify.
keyPoints: (i) Reflexive: a|a; Antisymmetric: if a|b and b|a then a=b; Transitive: if a|b and b|c then a|c
(ii) Hasse diagram: 1 at bottom, covers nothing; 2,3,4 covered by 1; 6 covered by 2 and 3; 12 covered by 4 and 6
(iii) Minimal: 1; Maximal: 12
(iv) Least: 1; Greatest: 12
(v) Yes, it is a lattice - every pair has LUB (LCM) and GLB (GCD)

questions.question: Define a lattice. Prove that in a lattice, the join and meet operations satisfy: (i) Commutative laws (ii) Associative laws (iii) Absorption laws (iv) Idempotent laws.
keyPoints: Lattice definition: poset where every pair has LUB (join) and GLB (meet)
(i) Commutative: a∨b = b∨a, a∧b = b∧a (by definition of LUB/GLB)
(ii) Associative: (a∨b)∨c = a∨(b∨c), similarly for meet - use LUB/GLB properties
(iii) Absorption: a∨(a∧a) = a, a∧(a∨a) = a - prove using properties of LUB/GLB
(iv) Idempotent: a∨a = a, a∧a = a - directly from definition

questions.question: (a) What is a chain and an antichain in a poset? (b) Find all chains and antichains in the poset (P({a,b}), ⊆). (c) State Dilworth's Theorem. (d) What is the width of the poset of divisors of 30 ordered by divisibility?
keyPoints: (a) Chain: subset where all elements comparable; Antichain: subset where no two elements comparable
(c) Dilworth's Theorem: In any finite poset, the size of the largest antichain equals the minimum number of chains needed to cover all elements
(d) Width of divisors of 30 = 3 (antichain {2,3,5} or {6,10,15} are maximal antichains)
